stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: New Hungary PM signals shift away from Kremlin after massive drone attack on Ukraine
stories.summary: Péter Magyar says his government has summoned Russian ambassador over drone attack near Hungary’s border to ask when Vladimir Putin plans ‘to finally end this bloody war’. What we know on day 1,541 Hungarian prime minister Péter Magyar says his new government has summoned the Russian ambassador over a massive drone attack near Hungary’s border in a significant shift from his predecessor’s friendly relations with Moscow . “The Hungarian government strongly condemns the Russian attack on Transcarp

stories.title: Draghi: Europe is ‘truly alone together’ in the age of Trump
stories.summary: Former European Central Bank President Mario Draghi delivered a stark warning to Europe in Aachen, Germany, on Thursday. “For the first time in living memory, we are truly alone together,” Draghi said as he accepted the International Charlemagne Prize, which rewards people for their contribution to European unity. Draghi doubled down on his message that Europe needs to get its act together to meet a new geopolitical reality — one where the U.S. has become “more adversarial and

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran against weaponising Hormuz to 'blackmail' Gulf countries
stories.summary: Qatar warned Tehran on Tuesday against using the Strait of Hormuz as a "weapon" to hold Gulf states to ransom, as Doha and Ankara backed ongoing mediation efforts to end the Iran war and restore access to the vital waterway.

stories.title: Philippine politician wanted by ICC flees senate after days holed up in building
stories.summary: Ronald dela Rosa, wanted over involvement in Duterte’s ‘war on drugs’, reportedly left heavily guarded building before dawn A Philippine lawmaker wanted by the international criminal court for his alleged role enforcing Rodrigo Duterte’s bloody anti-drugs crackdown has secretly fled the senate after spending days holed up in the building to avoid arrest. How do they criticize the US president’s policies without facing a social media riot, or losing their career ? stories.summary: Streeting calls on PM to resign and implies that he would stand as a candidate in a leadership contest Al Carns , the defence minister first elected in 2024, will launch his own leadership bid if a contest starts, Sky News is reporting . stories.summary: Docked ship reportedly seized outside UAE port by “unauthorised personnel” The Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, has said ships entering the strait of Hormuz must cooperate with the Iranian navy as reports emerged of a ship being seized outside a United Arab Emirate port and taken towards Iranian waters. stories.title: Latvian prime minister resigns amid row over drone incursions

Evika Siliņa announced her resignation on Thursday, a day after the Progressives party, her left-leaning coalition partner, withdrew its support over her

stories.title: Met chief says British Jews ‘not safe’ in London after series of attacks
stories.summary: King Charles visits Golders Green to show support as commissioner says counter-terrorism team leading 11 investigations Counter-terrorism officers in London have launched 11 investigations and arrested 35 people after “a sustained period of attack” upon the Jewish community, the head of the UK’s biggest police force has disclosed. stories.title: Latvian prime minister quits after backlash over stray Ukrainian drone strikes
stories.summary: Latvia’s center-right Prime Minister Evika Siliņa said Thursday she would resign, after her left-wing coalition partner withdrew its support for her in response to the firing of one of its ministers. “I am stepping down, but I am not giving up,” she said on X. The government will continue its duties until a new Cabinet of Ministers is approved. Siliņa had been serving as Prime Minister since September 2023. The left-wing Progressives party said Wednesday it would no longer support the coalition

stories.title: UK must drop ‘red lines’ for real EU reset, Brussels warns
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s plans for a major reset with the EU risk falling flat if he doesn’t relax his negotiating “red lines.” The embattled U.K. Labour leader vowed to put his country “at the heart of Europe,” as part of efforts to shore up support and shrug off calls for his resignation , following dismal local election results. stories.title: UN urges Equatorial Guinea to halt plans to return US deportees to home countries
stories.summary: Human rights experts make rare public appeal as US deportees describe being held in ‘prison-like’ conditions Human rights experts at the United Nations issued a rare public appeal to Equatorial Guinea, urging the West African country to halt its plans to return US deportees to their home countries where they face political violence, torture and death. The statement , cosigned by a representative of the African Commission on Human and People’s rights, adds diplomatic pressure on Equatorial Guinea

stories.title: Peace activists rally to protect Palestinians during Jerusalem flag march
stories.summary: Israeli peace activists gathered in Jerusalem on Thursday to support Palestinian residents ahead of the annual Jerusalem Day flag march, an event often marked by nationalist tensions and heavy security.

stories.title: Cartel corruption claims push US-Mexico relations to breaking point

stories.title: Trump invites Xi to Washington in September
stories.summary: President Donald Trump invited China’s leader Xi Jinping and his wife, Peng Liyuan, for a White House visit on Sept. 24. “We look forward to it,” Trump said during remarks at a banquet in Beijing. The president called the high-stakes summit Thursday a “fantastic day” and “historic,” adding that the meetings were “extremely positive and productive.” Xi and Trump appeared friendly and positive during their banquet remarks, praising one another’s leadership and vision. stories.title: Benjamin Netanyahu says he made secret trip to UAE at height of Iran war
stories.summary: Emirates’ foreign ministry rejects claims that Netanyahu visited country, describing them as ‘baseless’ Benjamin Netanyahu has claimed he made a secret trip to the United Arab Emirates at the height of the Iran war to meet the president, Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. “This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night. Continue reading...

stories.title: Brazil’s Atlantic forest records lowest deforestation in 40 years
stories.summary: Environmentalists hail decline but warn weakened laws could reverse gains Brazil’s Atlantic forest, the country’s most threatened biome, last year recorded its lowest level of deforestation since monitoring began 40 years ago, a new report shows. The forest is Brazil’s most populous biome, and home to 80% of the population and major cities such as Rio de Janeiro and São Paulo. In 2025 it recorded 8,658 hectares of deforestation, marking the first time it has fallen below 10,000 hectares since 19

stories.title: Republicans’ plan for zero state income tax could be ‘devastating’, experts warn
stories.summary: As Missouri asks voters whether to eliminate tax, experts say claims it will grow businesses and create jobs aren’t true Hannah Rejali, 34, lived through the failed so-called “Kansas Experiment” in the 2010s, when the Republican governor cut the state’s income tax to try to give a “ shot of adrenaline ” to its economy but instead left the state with a $900m budget shortfall. That meant, for example, that in 2015, at least eight school districts ended their academic year early . Continue reading.

stories.title: Salmon farm faces new cruelty claims as Trump seeks to supersize fish farming
stories.summary: New undercover video appears to show cruel treatment of salmon at Cooke hatchery amid push for ‘chickenification’ of fish The Trump administration is keen to do to fish what has been done to chickens – mass-produce them on an industrial scale to accelerate the US’s output of seafood. But this “chickenification” of fish may come at a hefty cost to the environment and to the fish themselves, as a new undercover video at one of the country’s leading fish farms has highlighted. stories.title: Miami residents sue over land for Trump presidential library
stories.summary: US plaintiffs say waterfront site was improperly transferred for Trump’s personal gain A group of Miami residents has filed a lawsuit against Donald Trump and the state of Florida over a land giveaway for his proposed presidential library . Almost three acres of prime waterfront land that once belonged to Miami Dade College (MDC) were illegally gifted to the US president by Florida’s Republican governor, Ron DeSantis, the lawsuit states. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canadian officer accused of spying for China acquitted of charges
stories.summary: William Majcher was accused of helping Chinese police coerce a Vancouver-area real estate investor, accused of fraud, to return to China A retired police officer Canada accused of being an agent for China has been acquitted of national security charges after prosecutors failed to prove he acted illegally. stories.title: ‘Magical’ objects from iron age hoard found in UK go on display
stories.summary: Exhibition of Melsonby hoard in York challenges ideas about life in northern Britain 2,000 years ago Iron age objects that tell a dramatic story of female power and which dispel the myth that northern Britain was a left-behind backwater have gone on display for the first time. The objects exhibited in York are from the Melsonby hoard, the largest trove of iron age metalwork ever found in the UK , which experts say could alter our understanding of life in Britain 2,000 years ago. stories.title: Potential UK leadership contender Angela Rayner cleared in tax probe

stories.title: Streeting quits Starmer’s Cabinet with blast at leadership ‘vacuum’ — live updates
stories.summary: Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned from Keir Starmer’s government Thursday — but is so far keeping his powder dry on a leadership challenge to the embattled British prime minister. Starmer, whose Labour Party swept to power on a landslide win less than two years ago, is reeling from disastrous election results in England, Wales and Scotland last week. stories.title: EU proposes end to ‘five tabs, three apps and a prayer’ for cross-border train bookings
stories.summary: New rules would enable single-ticket bookings across multiple rail operators throughout Europe Cross-border train journeys through several European countries are the stuff of many a holidaymaker’s dreams. But the reality of trying to buy the tickets, navigating multiple websites without knowing who can help if a connection is missed, can prove less than relaxing. As one MEP puts it, it can often require “five tabs, three apps and a prayer”. Continue reading...

stories.title: The Guardian view on the king’s speech: an agenda for a government that lacks conviction | Editorial
stories.summary: Keir Starmer’s programme is fatally limited by the timidity of an election manifesto that shied away from hard arguments Ending 14 years of Conservative rule was supposed to bring an end to dysfunctional government. In the speech that launched his 2024 general election campaign , Sir Keir Starmer said that “a vote for Labour is a vote for stability … a vote to stop the chaos”. stories.summary: All Her Lives is only the fifth short story collection to win the prestigious NZ$65,000 prize in 58 years First-time fiction writer Ingrid Horrocks has won New Zealand’s richest literary prize for her debut short story collection, All Her Lives . stories.summary: Tens of thousands of protesters marched through Brussels during a national strike called by Belgian unions against the government’s fiscal reforms.

After 25 years, I can’t help but go back Nothing prepares you for the shock that is the Cannes film festival: the adrenaline, the fatigue, the elation and the emotion, but also the hunger, the anger, the magic and the ridicule. stories.title: US man accused of killing rock singer in 1993 arrested after decades on the run
stories.summary: Richard Werstine, wanted in connection with killing of Cold as Life vocalist Rodney Barger, was arrested in Panama A suspect accused of murdering his rock singer friend in 1993 has been apprehended in Central America after spending more than 30 years successfully evading authorities. According to the US Marshals Service, Richard Werstine, who was wanted in connection with the killing of Cold as Life vocalist Rodney Barger, was arrested in Panama. Continue reading...

stories.title: Peacocks take over northern Italian beach town
stories.summary: More than 100 peacocks now roam Punta Marina in northern Italy, leaving residents searching for humane ways to manage the birds.

stories.title: Kazakhstan launches driverless Light Rail Transit system after 10 years of delays
stories.summary: After a decade of construction delays, Astana has launched its Light Rail Transit (LRT) system, featuring driverless overground trains serving 18 stations across the city.

stories.title: SOCAR acquisition of Italy’s Italiana Petroli finalised after EU clearance
stories.summary: SOCAR has completed its acquisition of Italiana Petroli after securing all regulatory approvals, marking a major expansion of its downstream presence in Italy and strengthening its position in Europe’s energy market.

stories.title: Palestinians gather to mark 78th anniversary of the Nakba and call for 'right of return'
stories.summary: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank and the Gaza Strip marked the 78th anniversary of the Nakba, a defining moment in Palestinian history dating back to 1948, when hundreds of thousands of people were forced to flee their homes, villages and land, becoming refugees.

stories.title: Pentagon says US has spent $29 billion on Iran war so far, amid concerns over weapons stockpiles
stories.summary: Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth came under intense questioning by Democratic and Republican lawmakers over the cost of war, Trump's end game and diminishing weapons stockpiles.

stories.title: China’s Unitree unveils transformable humanoid ‘mecha’ robot with cockpit
stories.summary: Chinese robotics company Unitree Robotics unveiled a transformable humanoid “mecha” robot featuring a cockpit for a human pilot.

stories.title: Trump arrives in Beijing for high-stakes summit with Xi
stories.summary: This week's trip, the first since Trump visited Beijing in 2017, will involve highly anticipated talks with Xi on Thursday and Friday.

stories.title: Barcelona star Lamine Yamal waves Palestinian flag during La Liga celebrations
stories.summary: Footage released on May 12 showed Yamal waving a Palestinian flag during FC Barcelona’s La Liga title parade celebrating the club’s 29th league title. In a news conference, Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said he did not like such gestures, but added that Yamal was old enough to make his own decisions.

stories.title: Russian cargo ship sunk off Spain carried nuclear reactors for North Korea, probe reveals
stories.summary: Spanish investigators have discovered that the Russian cargo ship that sank in the Mediterranean in 2024 — originally believed to be part of a weapons retrieval mission to Syria — was secretly carrying nuclear reactor components bound for North Korea.

stories.title: Cannes Film Festival 2026: Fans lock down red carpet spots ahead of opening
stories.summary: Film fans gather overnight at Cannes, using stepladders and chains to secure red carpet spots as they wait for a glimpse of opening night stars.

stories.title: Australian property developer says Trump hotel plan scrapped due to 'toxic' brand
stories.summary: The Australian developer's statement followed a report in the Australian Financial Review that the Trump Organisation had pulled out of the deal.

stories.title: Iran's economy is being tested by war, blockades and soaring inflation
stories.summary: Inflation has hit 54% and the rial has lost more than half its value, but Tehran insists it can hold out

stories.title: Watch: UK Parliament revives centuries-old ritual before King’s Speech
stories.summary: Black Rod was symbolically denied entry to the House of Commons during the ceremonial opening of the UK Parliament ahead of King Charles III’s speech.

stories.title: A weakened Trump to test China's appetite for deal-making
stories.summary: A quagmire in Iran, sky-high oil prices and shrinking support at home: when US President Donald Trump belatedly travels to China this week, his position has weakened. Still, he is hoping for any deal. Nervous Europeans are watching.

stories.title: Yes, Europe’s leaders are finally standing up to Trump. Here’s why | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz’s criticism of the US president was not a solo run. It was born of the realisation that US leverage has slipped Friedrich Merz’s criticism of Donald Trump last month reflected more than a moment of personal candour or a split between Berlin and the White House. It pointed to a broader shift under way among European leaders. Increasingly they are willing to publicly confront the Trump administration on issues ranging from Iran to Ukraine and European sovereignty. The Trump administ

stories.title: Gaborone gold rush: how Botswana rose to the top of men’s sprinting
stories.summary: Country with a population of just 2.5m credits investment in young athletes for its rise but this progress is under threat It was a fairytale ending to the World Athletics Relays in Gaborone. In the final strait, Collen Kebinatshipi surged past South Africa’s Zakithi Nene to win the men’s 4x400m relay for Botswana. The home crowd, a sea of light blue, went wild. “It means so many things to us,” Letsile Tebogo, 22, the reigning 200m Olympic champion, who ran the second leg, told reporters afterwa

stories.title: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict at record high in 2025
stories.summary: The 32.3m surpasses those caused by disasters for the first time, as 82.2m people displaced in total around world The number of internal displacements triggered by conflict or violence around the world reached a record high in 2025, surpassing the number of disaster-driven internal displacements for the first time. A report published by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC) shows that by the end of 2025 there were 32.3m conflict-driven internal displacements. That is 60% higher than

stories.title: German ultrarunner Arda Saatçi completes 600-kilometre Death Valley to Los Angeles challenge
stories.summary: German athlete Arda Saatçi completed a 600-kilometre ultramarathon challenge from Death Valley to Los Angeles after running for more than 123 hours.

stories.title: Final passengers disembark hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
stories.summary: The final passengers aboard the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius disembarked in Tenerife before boarding flights to quarantine facilities in more than 20 countries.

stories.title: Macron thanks Kipchoge for 'patience' after Nairobi morning run
stories.summary: Macron joined marathon legend Eliud Kipchoge for an early morning run in Nairobi on day three of his Africa tour, later thanking the two-time Olympic champion for his "patience."

stories.title: At least nine killed after bomb rigged to rickshaw explodes at north Pakistan bazaar
stories.summary: The Pakistani Taliban and other militant groups have grown more emboldened since the Afghan Taliban returned to power in Kabul in 2021.

stories.title: Iran threatens weapons-grade uranium enrichment as peace talks falter
stories.summary: Tehran threatened to enrich uranium to weapons-grade if attacked again, as Trump said the ceasefire had a "one percent chance" of surviving after rejecting Tehran's proposal.

stories.title: Chinese people divided ahead of Trump-Xi summit in Beijing
stories.summary: Residents in Beijing shared mixed opinions ahead of the upcoming meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with some hoping for improved ties and others expressing skepticism.

stories.title: Trump ally Roger Stone condemned for providing lobbying services to Myanmar’s military junta
stories.summary: Stone being paid $50,000 a month to ‘rebuild’ relations between Washington and Myanmar’s military-backed government The US lobbyist Roger Stone, a longtime friend and ally of Donald Trump, has been condemned for accepting $50,000 a month to “rebuild” relations between Washington and Myanmar’s military-backed government. Myanmar’s leaders have been internationally isolated since seizing power in a coup in 2021 , and have repeatedly been accused of atrocities that may amount to war crimes . Activi

stories.title: Putin says Russia will prevail at scaled-down Victory Day parade – video
stories.summary: Russia held its smallest Victory Day parade in years on Saturday due to security concerns over Ukraine drone strikes. The parade on Red Square marks the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany and was once an opportunity to show off Russia's vast military hardware and nuclear missiles, but this year there were no tanks rolling over the cobbles of the Moscow square Russia will always be victorious, says Putin at scaled-back Victory Day parade Continue reading...

stories.title: Angela Rayner calls blocking Andy Burnham’s return to parliament a mistake as pressure mounts on Keir Starmer – as it happened
stories.summary: Catherine West issues ultimatum for PM as ex-minister Josh Simons joins calls for prime minister to quit At the start of her programme Laura Kuenssberg addressed Catherine West and Bridget Phillipson who were sitting waiting for the main interviews. Kuenssberg told West she wanted a cabinet minister to challenge Keir Starmer. She said she was sitting next to one of them. What was her message to her? Well, there’s nothing stopping Bridget from standing. Why are all the men better than the women?

stories.title: EU preparing to offer key concession to UK in new post-Brexit agricultural deal
stories.summary: Exclusive: Britain expected to be allowed to keep ban on live animal exports, sources say, in fillip for Keir Starmer UK politics live – latest updates Brussels is preparing to offer Keir Starmer a key concession in talks over an agricultural deal, giving the beleaguered prime minister an important victory in his efforts to move closer to the EU. European officials have conceded that the UK can keep its ban on live animal exports as part of any joint deal on food and agricultural products, accor

stories.title: Future of UK lies in the EU after Brexit ‘failure’, say European Greens
stories.summary: Exclusive: Co-chair Vula Tsetsi says it is time to trigger debate, as statement is agreed at annual leadership meeting in Brussels The European Green party has urged the UK to consider rejoining the EU and draw a line under the “political and economic failure” of Brexit. A text declaring that “the United Kingdom’s future lies in the European Union” was adopted by a large majority of European green parties at the movement’s annual leadership meeting on Friday, the day before Europe Day. Continue

stories.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers 'remain asymptomatic' before flights home – video
stories.summary: Spanish health minister Monica Garcia says all those onboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship were 'asymptomatic' after it arrived in Tenerife in the Canary Islands on Sunday. Everyone disembarking will be checked for symptoms, authorities say, and be taken off the ship only once evacuation flights are ready to fly them to their destinations Argentina in spotlight over hantavirus as authorities retrace footsteps of ship’s passengers Dozens of people from cruise ship struck by hantavirus leave T

stories.title: Britain and Ireland’s wildflowers – in pictures
stories.summary: The Eden Project’s National Wildflower Centre is opening entries for its Wildflower Photographer of the Year 2026 competition on 29 May. The contest showcases images of some of Britain and Ireland’s 1,600 wildflower species, and a selection of photos from last year’s competition will be on display at Eden Dock, Canary Wharf, London, during CWG’s Nature Week, from 13 July Continue reading...

stories.title: Lib Dems accuse Badenoch of being willing to ‘put Farage in No 10’ after she hints she would approve council pacts – as it happened
stories.summary: Conservative leader says her party ‘are willing to work with people who will help deliver Conservative policies’ Labour has criticised Zack Polanski for saying he was concerned about how the suspect in the Golders Green stabbings was treated when he was arrested by the police. Referring to what Polanski said about this in his Today interview this morning (see 9.33am ), a Labour party spokesperson said: Our brave police ran towards a suspected terrorist and tackled him while he was still carrying

stories.title: Tensions high in West Bengal after BJP aide shot dead and hundreds arrested
stories.summary: Turmoil and violence rocks state after prime minister Narendra Modi’s party claimed victory in legislative election Tensions have been high in the Indian state of West Bengal after a top political aide from Narendra Modi’s party was shot dead in the street and hundreds were arrested as violence broke out following elections this week. The prime minister’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) claimed victory in the West Bengal elections on Monday , defeating Trinamool Congress (TMC), which had ruled over

Shock in India after man takes remains of his sister to bank to prove her death
stories.summary: Jitu Munda says he was refused access to money in case highlighting ‘lack of humanity’ in Indian bureaucracy The sight of a man bringing the remains of his dead sister to a bank in India after officials had refused to let him withdraw money without proof of her death has caused shock in India. Jitu Munda, 52, from the Indian state of Odisha, was captured on video carrying the remains of his recently deceased sister through the streets of Keonjhar and placing them outside the local bank. Continue

stories.title: Narendra Modi’s BJP wins election in West Bengal for the first time
stories.summary: Result in key Indian state is set to have significant implications for the country’s political landscape Narendra Modi’s party has won a resounding election victory in West Bengal, a state which had been a rare opposition stronghold, expanding his unrivalled consolidation of power across the country. It is the first time that the Indian prime minister’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) has won assembly elections in West Bengal, a large and politically significant state in eastern India. Continue rea

stories.title: ‘Defeated by conspiracy’: West Bengal chief minister refuses to resign after election loss
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee says Narendra Modi’s party ‘forcefully captured’ election that ended her party’s 15 years in power A political showdown is taking place in the Indian state of West Bengal as the chief minister, Mamata Banerjee, India’s most powerful female politician, has refused to resign after she lost elections to the prime minister’s party this week. Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) won an overwhelming victory on Monday in state elections in West Bengal, where Banerjee and her Tri

stories.title: Donate now to a Top 10 CNN Hero
stories.summary: Anderson Cooper explains how you can easily donate to any of the 2021 Top 10 CNN Heroes.

stories.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
stories.summary: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

stories.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
stories.summary: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

stories.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
stories.summary: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

stories.title: CNN Heroes: Sharing the Spotlight
The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
stories.summary: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

stories.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
stories.summary: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

stories.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
stories.summary: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

stories.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
stories.summary: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

stories.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
stories.summary: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

stories.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
stories.summary: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

stories.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
stories.summary: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

stories.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
stories.summary: Paul Templer was living his best life.

stories.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
stories.summary: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

stories.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
stories.summary: This is it.

stories.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
stories.summary: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

stories.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
stories.summary: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

stories.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
stories.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

stories.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
stories.summary: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.
